Off Calendar Jun-13-2024 Continued To Aug-28-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT SPOTON TRANSACT, LLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT

SpotOn Transact, LLC's demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

San Francisco-based SpotOn sued three former employees who allegedly started competitive ventures while working for SpotOn. The former employees cross-claimed for violations of California wage and hour laws and invoked California's Private Attorney Generals Act (PAGA). It is undisputed, however, that the former employees worked and lived outside California when employed by SpotOn, so the demurrer's focus is whether California wage and hour laws applied to them. They did not.

A key case - cited by both sides - is Oman v. Delta Airlines, Inc. (2023) 9 Cal.5th 762. In it, the California Supreme found that employees who do not spend a majority of their working time in California are not governed by the state's wage and hour laws, stating: "we ordinarily presume the Legislature drafts laws with domestic conditions in mind." (Id. at 773.) In our case, the three employees do not claim to have spent any working time in California.

The employees briefly attempt to distinguish Oman by noting their employment agreements were "governed by the laws of the State of California." (Opp. 6:18-7:1.) But they tip their hand by urging leave to amend to plead their home states' wage and hour laws. (Id. at 7:2-8:9.)
Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
